display._sbs_height_note: SBS panel height: 1080 (default) or 1200 for the Viture Beast's full native panel.

cameras._fps_note: OWLsight fps should match or exceed display.target_fps (90) to minimise camera-to-photon latency — sampling slower than the display wastes up to one frame interval of frame age. fps is a best-effort FrameDurationLimits request; libcamera clamps to the nearest rate the sensor supports at this resolution, so a value the sensor can't hit degrades gracefully rather than failing.
cameras._rotation_note: rotation_deg snaps to {0, 90, 180, 270}; applied as UV math in the NV12 vertex shader (no readback, ~free). Use when the CSI camera is mounted sideways/upside-down on the helmet. Settable live from Cameras > Left/Right Camera > Rotation.
cameras._csi_retry_note: A CSI sensor that comes up wedged at boot leaves an eye dark until reboot. csi_boot_retries auto-attempts a reinit that many times early at startup (also available manually via Vision > CSI Camera Controls > Reinitialize CSI Cameras, or the quick-menu Diagnostics item). csi_expected is how many CSI eyes you have (set 1 for a mono build so it never retries a second camera you don't have).
cameras._eye_source_note: Valid values: csi | usb1 | usb2 | usb3. Routes that camera feed as the fullscreen background for the given eye.
cameras._multicam_note: multicam_layout = true overrides the per-eye source pickers and renders the same quad composite in both eyes: CSI left/right (rotated per Cameras > Left/Right Camera > Rotation) side-by-side on the top half, multicam_usb_a + multicam_usb_b side-by-side on the bottom. Set the CSI rotation to 90° (clockwise) for the intended portrait fit on the top half.

lora._note: RAK4631 enumerates as ACM2 (after Teensy=ACM0, SmartKnob=ACM1). Adjust if udev assigns a different index.

wireless_controller._note: ESP32-C3 USB-serial bridge for ESP-NOW in-paw controller. Set enabled:true and adjust port once the bridge is connected.

gpio._about: Configurable GPIO switch map. Each entry in 'pins' assigns a BCM pin a short-press and optional long-press function, with pull bias ('up'|'down'|'none') and polarity (active_low=true means a momentary switch wired to GND). Edit in-HUD via System > GPIO Buttons; toggling Enabled applies instantly and 'Apply Changes Now' reloads the poller after slot edits (no relaunch needed). Functions: none, boop_snout, boop_left, boop_right, boop_both, menu_open, menu_select, menu_back, system_restart, system_shutdown, cam_af_left, cam_af_right, cam_pip_left, cam_pip_right, cam_capture_left, cam_capture_right, cam_swap, phone_ring.

coprocessor._about: OPTIONAL button/switch coprocessor (RP2350/RP2040). When enabled, an external MCU debounces the switches and streams presses to the Pi, dispatched through the same functions as the GPIO map above. Never required — leave disabled to use on-board GPIO only. Toggle live in System > GPIO Buttons > Button Coprocessor. See docs/coprocessor-input.md.
coprocessor._transport_note: 'usb_serial' (USB CDC, default) or 'i2c' (specified but not implemented in v1).
coprocessor._device_note: Use a stable /dev/serial/by-id/... path matched on the coprocessor's USB serial string — NOT /dev/ttyACMn, which races with the Teensy/SmartKnob/RAK4631.
coprocessor._replace_note: false = additive (GPIO slots + coproc both fire). true = coproc is the only button source (local GPIO poller is not started).
coprocessor._buttons_note: Maps the coprocessor's button ids to functions (same function ids as gpio.pins). The MCU reports 'button N, short/long'; the Pi decides what N means, so remapping stays here.

pip._anchor_values: top_left | top_center | top_right | bottom_left | bottom_center | bottom_right

protoface._about: Protoface LED face. mode 'daemon' (default) talks to the Python daemon over socket+shm; mode 'native' renders the face in-process in ProtoHUD (no daemon) and writes the same /dev/shm frame, so the preview and scripts/panel_driver.py work unchanged. autostart (daemon mode) launches the daemon on boot. preview is the on-glasses panel-preview placement (persisted on exit).
protoface._expression_effects_note: When true, the active particle effect auto-swaps to a mood preset as the face expression changes (angry->fire, happy->celebration, sad->rain, shocked->galaxy); neutral/unmapped faces restore your chosen effect. Toggle live in Face > Effects > Expression Effects.
protoface._panel_driver_note: native mode only: auto-launch scripts/panel_driver.py to drive the HUB75 panels. Set false for preview-only or if you run the driver yourself.
protoface._native_about: native-mode options: assets_dir points at the Protoface checkout holding faces/materials/gifs (default $HOME/protohud/Protoface); panels overrides the default 2-panel mirrored layout.

preview._view_values: 0 = whole face | 1 = left half | 2 = right half

scheduler._about: On-board scheduler/reminders. Networking (phone web form + Google Calendar) lives in the scheduler_daemon Python companion, which writes events.json + scheduler_status.json. ProtoHUD reads those by file poll. Paths default to ~/.local/share/protohud-scheduler/ on both sides (leave events_path/status_path unset unless you need a custom location).

kdeconnect._about: Bridges phone notifications from KDE Connect (over LAN or USB tether) into the HUD's notification queue. Requires kdeconnectd running on the Pi and the phone paired via the KDE Connect Android/iOS app. Bridge is gated by libdbus-1-dev being available at build time; on platforms without DBus the section is silently ignored.
kdeconnect._device_id_note: Empty picks the first paired+reachable device. Set to a specific id (see kdeconnect-cli --list-devices) when multiple phones are paired.
kdeconnect._message_apps_note: CSV of appName substrings treated as chat/DMs: these get a larger toast (sender as the title, message wrapped below) that stays up ~14 s so you can read who sent it and what it says.
kdeconnect._ignore_list_note: CSV of substrings matched against a notification's title + text (case-insensitive). Anything matching is dropped — use it to mute noisy Discord servers or group chats by name.

phone_inbox._about: Watches a directory for files dropped in from the phone (KDE Connect's Share & Receive lands them in ~/Downloads by default). New face PNGs / GIFs surface an Import toast in the HUD; accept moves the file to <watch_dir>/.processed/, dismiss moves it to <watch_dir>/.dismissed/.
phone_inbox._watch_dir_note: Empty defaults to $XDG_DOWNLOAD_DIR / ~/Downloads. Point this at a dedicated dir if you want a cleaner inbox (then change KDE Connect's Share destination to match).
phone_inbox._auto_dismiss_note: 0 keeps the import toast sticky until you act on it. Setting it would let untouched files quietly skip import, which is usually not what you want.

notifications._about: The notification log (Communications > Notification Log) keeps the last 50 notifications, browseable and filterable by type/sender. persist saves it to protohud_notifications.json next to config.json so a sudden reboot doesn't lose them; toggle it live under Communications > Notification Log > Persist Log.

audio._note: Audio capture is handled by the RP2350 helmet audio processor (USB Audio UAC2). The CM5 receives pre-processed stereo and routes it to the chosen output. Run 'arecord -l' to verify the RP2350 card name after connecting via USB.

android._about: Android device mirror via scrcpy → V4L2 loopback → OpenCV.
android._prereqs: sudo apt install scrcpy v4l2loopback-dkms && sudo modprobe v4l2loopback video_nr=4 card_label=AndroidMirror exclusive_caps=1
android._anchor_values: top_left | top_center | top_right | bottom_left | bottom_center | bottom_right

mpu9250._about: HiLetgo GY-9250 / MPU-9250 backup compass via I2C. Wired to CM5 40-pin header: SDA=GPIO2(pin3), SCL=GPIO3(pin5), VCC=3.3V(pin1), GND(pin6). AD0 to GND for address 0x68.
mpu9250._mpu_addr_note: 104 = 0x68 (AD0 low). Use 105 (0x69) if AD0 tied high.
mpu9250._declination_note: Local magnetic declination in degrees. Positive = East, negative = West. Find yours at ngdc.noaa.gov/geomag/calculators/magcalc.shtml
mpu9250._heading_offset_note: Mechanical mounting offset in degrees. Adjust so north reads 0 when sensor points north.
mpu9250._mag_bias_note: Hard-iron calibration offsets. Auto-updated by Calibrate Compass in the menu.

bno055._about: Adafruit BNO055 9-DOF absolute orientation sensor via I2C. On-chip sensor fusion — heading comes from the chip directly (no software AHRS needed) and stays stable through tilt. Preferred over MPU9250 when both are present (Auto IMU source picks BNO055 first). Wired to CM5 40-pin header: SDA=GPIO2(pin3), SCL=GPIO3(pin5), VCC=3.3V(pin1), GND(pin6). ADR pin floating/low = 0x28; ADR tied high = 0x29.
bno055._clock_stretch_note: The BNO055 stretches the I2C clock, which the Pi/RP1 hardware I2C handles poorly — it can read fine on an Arduino but be flaky/undetectable on the Pi. If `i2cdetect -y 1` doesn't show 28, or readings are unstable, slow the bus in /boot/firmware/config.txt: `dtparam=i2c_arm_baudrate=10000` (10 kHz). Alternatively use a software bus: `dtoverlay=i2c-gpio,bus=3,...` and set i2c_bus to /dev/i2c-3.
bno055._i2c_addr_note: 40 = 0x28 (default). Use 41 (0x29) if the ADR jumper is closed.
bno055._declination_note: Local magnetic declination in degrees. Positive = East, negative = West. Find yours at ngdc.noaa.gov/geomag/calculators/magcalc.shtml
bno055._heading_offset_note: Mechanical mounting offset in degrees. Adjust so north reads 0 when sensor points north.
bno055._heading_axes_note: Axis remap for non-default mounting. 0 = factory default (X-forward, Y-left, Z-up). The chip's calibration is preserved across axis remaps so re-calibration isn't needed when this changes.
bno055._poll_hz_note: Sensor read rate. 50 Hz matches the chip's internal fusion rate; higher just resamples the same fused value.
bno055._save_calibration_note: Persist the chip's calibration profile to bno055_calib.bin (next to this file) so it survives reboots. Restored automatically at startup; saved on demand via Compass > Save IMU Calibration, and auto-saved once the chip first reaches full calibration (sys 3/3) if no file exists yet. Calibrate by rotating the head through several orientations + a figure-8 for the magnetometer until all four values read 3.

video._note: Video recording. camera = left|right|both; fourcc is a 4-char OpenCV codec (mp4v widely available, avc1 needs H.264).

resolution._note: Persisted OWLsight resolution. Overrides cameras.owlsight_left/right width/height/fps on startup. fps set to match display.target_fps (90) for lowest latency; libcamera clamps to the sensor's achievable rate.

qr._note: Persisted QR scan state. scan_main=OWLsight cameras, scan_usb=USB cameras.

map._note2: The minimap module is always on (forced by hud_dock); 'enabled' is kept for compatibility.
map._note: map_path is the full path to the last-used map image. pan_x/y and calibration are reset each session.

info_panel._about: Cycling info panel — an opposite-side twin of the minimap (same footprint). Position comes from hud_dock; size mirrors the map. 'show' is [clock, notifications, schedule, weather-now, weather-precip]. clock_face: 0=ticks, 1=numbers, 2=minimal.

hud_dock._about: Docks the minimap (always right) and info panel (always left). bottom: false=top edge, true=bottom edge. v_offset: fine vertical nudge in pixels (Location menu Up/Down).

weather._about: Weather widget (Open-Meteo, no API key; fetched via curl). auto_locate uses IP geolocation; otherwise set lat/lon. metric: true=C/kph, false=F/mph. Needs the device online + curl installed.

splash._image_note: PNG (RGBA preferred). Relative paths resolve from the binary directory; absolute paths used as-is. Example: "assets/branding/logo.png".
splash.title: PROTOHUD
splash.subtitle: XR HUD System
